projectDescription: A smarter cd command that learns your most-used directories and lets you jump to them with just a few keystrokes.
keyFeatures: Frecency-based directory ranking that learns from usage patterns
Works with all major shells: Bash, Zsh, Fish, PowerShell, Nushell, Elvish, Xonsh, Tcsh, and POSIX
Interactive fuzzy selection with fzf integration
Import data from autojump, fasd, z, z.lua, and zsh-z
Configurable via environment variables and init flags
Cross-platform: Linux, macOS, Windows, BSD, and Android
publicApiSurface: z <keywords> — jump to highest-ranked matching directory
zi <keywords> — interactive jump with fzf selection
zoxide add <path> — add a directory to the database
zoxide remove <path> — remove a directory from the database
zoxide import --from=<autojump|z> <path> — import from another tool
_ZO_EXCLUDE_DIRS — glob patterns of directories to exclude
_ZO_MAXAGE — maximum total age for database entries
